args: Unified CLI for training and tuning. Dispatches to train, texel, texel-weights, data, convert, pgn2texel.
Extract fen,result CSV from PGN (one position per game at a fixed ply).
Map PGN Result header (1-0, 0-1, 1/2-1/2, etc.) to float 1.0, 0.0, 0.5 or None.
float | None 1.0, 0.0, 0.5 or None if unrecognized.
Read PGN, sample one position per game at --ply, write fen,result CSV.
Convert .pt (from train) to legacy .nnue binary for this engine's nnue-probe.
Write a 32-bit unsigned integer in little-endian to the binary file.
Binary file handle open for writing.
Write a 16-bit signed integer in little-endian, clamped to int16 range.
Write an 8-bit signed integer, clamped to [-128, 127].
Write a 32-bit signed integer in little-endian.
Inverse of nnue-probe permute_biases so that after load the engine sees our order.
Scale a float weight to int16 using 2^scale_bits (e.g. 6 for Stockfish).
Load .pt file; return (state_dict, arch). arch is 'small_mlp' or 'halfkp'.
Registers input, output, and --description for the convert command.
Write the legacy nnue-probe binary: header, transformer block, network block (with identity 32->32 and inverse bias permutation), and padding to LEGACY_FILE_SIZE.
Binary file handle open for writing.
String stored in .nnue header (padded/truncated to 177 bytes).
Convert .pt to .nnue from parsed args: load state_dict, write legacy format.
HalfKP feature indices: 41024 per half (64*641). Index = king_sq*641 + piece_sq*10 + piece_type.
Parse FEN piece placement into list of (square, piece_type). Square 0-63, a8=0.
list[tuple[int, int]] List of (square, piece_type) for each non-king piece.
Compute HalfKP feature indices for a position. Returns (white_half_indices, black_half_indices), each list of 11 indices (bias + 10 non-king pieces) in [0, 41023].
Holds constants for the HalfKP feature set: FEATURES_PER_HALF (41024), STRIDE (641), TOTAL_FEATURES (82048).
Generate FEN,score_cp CSV by running the engine in UCI (go depth N) per position.
Run engine in UCI: position fen, go depth N; parse last info score cp/mate. Returns centipawn score (or large value for mate) or None on failure.
int | None Centipawn score or None.
Generate FEN,score_cp CSV: read FENs, run engine per position, write CSV.
PeSTO material + PST with game-phase interpolation; matches src/eval/pesto.c.
Flip square for black's perspective (a8<->a1 etc.).
Build mg_table[12][64] and eg_table[12][64] from piece values and PSTs (like pesto_init).
Parse FEN; return (board 8x8 of piece chars or '.', white_to_move).
Eval position from White's perspective (positive = good for White). Material + PST with game-phase interpolation; matches src/eval/pesto.c structure.
Tune PeSTO term weights (8 scale factors) from engine eval-breakdown-batch.
P(white wins) = 1 / (1 + exp(-k * x / 400)). x in centipawns.
Run engine eval-breakdown-batch; return (N, 8) array of term values per position.
Load fen,result; get breakdown from engine; minimize cross-entropy over 8 weights; write JSON.
Texel tuning: minimize cross-entropy between sigmoid(eval) and game result; tune mg_value/eg_value.
P(white wins) = 1 / (1 + exp(-k * x / 400)). x in centipawns from White's view.
Send one batch (params + FENs) to engine eval-batch; return array of scores (white cp).
Current mg/eg (and optionally k) for engine.
np.ndarray One score per FEN (White cp).
Negative sum of result*log(p) + (1-result)*log(1-p). Params are mg/eg values (and optionally k). If get_evals is set, use it; else use Python eval_fen.
Optional callable(params) -> evals; if None uses Python eval_fen.
Load fen,result CSV; run Texel tuning (L-BFGS-B or coordinate descent); write JSON and print C snippet.
Custom NNUE-style trainer. Reads CSV (fen, score_cp); supports mlp (768->256->32->1) and halfkp (HalfKP FT -> 512->32->1). Saves .pt for the convert command.
Convert FEN to a flat board vector of shape (768,). One-hot over 12*64; assumes FEN has at least the piece-placement field. Used only for the mlp architecture.
torch.Tensor Shape (768,) float32 one-hot board vector.
Load CSV with columns fen and score_cp. Returns list of board tensors and list of scores; skips invalid rows. Used when --arch mlp.
tuple[list[torch.Tensor], list[float]] Board tensors and scores.
MLP for mlp architecture: 768 -> 256 -> 32 -> 1. Dense board input; converted .nnue has zero feature transformer.
HalfKP NNUE: sparse indices -> two EmbeddingBags -> 512 -> 32 -> 1. Compatible with .nnue conversion when using --arch halfkp.
Creates ft_white, ft_black EmbeddingBags and fc2, fc3.
Flattened white-half feature indices (11 per sample).
Load CSV (fen, score_cp); return (white_indices, black_indices) per row and scores.
tuple[list[tuple[list[int], list[int]]], list[float]] List of (white_indices, black_indices) and scores.
ArgumentParser or subparser to add flags to.
Train from args: load data, build model, run epochs, save .pt.
